Perfect! Now I understand the complete picture. The issue is that test Plans with labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"] without "erk-core" will NOT be returned by default when the command invokes `pr list` without explicit --label flags, because the default is now ("erk-core",).
Let me now create a comprehensive report by summarizing my findings:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get line counts for main affected files

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now let me create my final comprehensive report:
## Summary Report: Label Mismatch Analysis for erk-core Label Change
Based on my thorough search and analysis of the test suite, I have identified all test files with potential label mismatches related to the new "erk-core" label default.
### Key Documentation Reference From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md`: - **erk-core** is the new label for core work (non-learn PRs) - **erk-plan** is the type label for implementation plans - **erk-pr** is the universal base label for all erk-submitted PRs - Default label filter for `pr list` command is now **("erk-core",)** (set at line 281 and 400 in list_cmd.py)
### Critical Finding: test_dash_workflow_runs.py
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_dash_workflow_runs.py`
This file has Plan objects that already include "erk-core" in their labels, which is correct: - Line 85: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` - Line 181: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` - Line 281: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]`
These tests are already properly updated and will work correctly with the new default.
### Remaining Test Files Needing Updates
All the following test files create Plan objects with `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` (missing "erk-core"):
**PR Command Tests (tests/commands/pr/):** 1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_view.py` - 14 occurrences 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_check_plan.py` - 12 occurrences 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_close.py` - 12 occurrences 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_log.py` - 6 occurrences 5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` - 4 occurrences 6. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_duplicate_check.py` - 2 occurrences 7. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py` - 2 occurrences 8. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py` - 1 occurrence
**Dashboard/Filtering Tests (tests/commands/dash/):** 9. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_filtering.py` - Plans with only ["erk-pr", "erk-plan"] at lines 32, 45, 81, 94 10. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_pr_columns.py` 11. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_impl_columns.py` 12. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_action_state.py` 13. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_workflow_run_state.py`
**Other Test Files:** 14. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/branch/test_checkout_cmd.py` 15. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/land/test_objective_update.py` 16. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/learn/test_display.py` 17. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/objective/test_plan.py` 18. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/objective/test_plan_one_shot.py` 19. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/run/test_list.py` 20. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_top_level_commands.py` 21. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/workspace/test_delete.py`
### Test Impact Analysis
**Risk Categories:**
1. **HIGH RISK - Tests that invoke `pr list` without explicit --label flags:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_filtering.py` (lines 63, 114, 215, 337) - Creates plans with `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` then calls `runner.invoke(cli, ["pr", "list"], obj=ctx)` - These plans will NOT be returned by default because the command filters by "erk-core"
2. **MEDIUM RISK - Tests that display or check plan details without filtering:** - `test_view.py` - Displays plan details for plans without erk-core - `test_check_plan.py` - Checks plan status - `test_close.py` - Closes plans without erk-core label - These use `pr view <number>` and `pr check <number>` which may directly fetch by ID rather than filtering
3. **LOW RISK - Tests that set up plans but may not rely on label filtering:** - Commands like `pr log`, `pr remote-paths`, `pr rewrite` that don't use default filtering
### Reference: Already Updated File
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_list.py`
This file has already been properly updated with all Plan objects containing the "erk-core" label: - Lines 43, 56, 89, 102, etc.: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]`
This is the correct pattern that other test files should follow.
